name: auth
description: The protocol layer that operates on the Identity substrate. Owns every authentication endpoint — login (Sanctum-backed), refresh (with rotation + reuse detection), logout, MFA challenge dispatch, password reset flow (forgot + reset), email verification confirmation, cross-app SSO grant issuance + exchange. Signs every inter-service JWT (HS256 with per-Application `app` claim binding + key rotation via `kid`) and publishes the JWKS endpoint with aggressive Cache-Control. Ships the CompliantAgePolicy service that resolves the effective age floor from the compliance chain (COPPA / GDPR per-country / framework default / tenant override — floor can only tighten). Ships the ScopeSuffixResolver service that the base repository (in framework/crud) consults to apply the `.own` / `.tenant` / `.branch` / `.team` / `.child` / `.any` filter suffixes per caller permission. Owns the RegistrationPolicyInterface contract + default GenericRegistrationPolicy implementation; domain modules (e.g. sports/athletes in Wave 7) bind their own implementations via #[Bind]. Refresh-token rotation with reuse detection (already-consumed refresh → invalidate all tokens for the User = breach signal). Sanctum PAT abilities cache the compact permission array at issue-time from RBAC. Session revocation on credential mutation (PasswordChanged listener revokes every active PAT for every User row referencing the Identity — cross-Application, one-action lockdown). This module NEVER stores credentials — it delegates every hash / verify / recovery-code operation to the `identity` substrate.

consumedIndirectlyBy.description: Every downstream tenant-plane module reaches this module ONLY via HTTP middleware (`auth:sanctum` guard + `auth.enforce_session_scope` + `auth.jwt_verify` for inter-service calls). Domain modules NEVER inject auth services directly — they inject User / PlatformUser resolved by the guard.

wraps.rationale: Sanctum's `personal_access_tokens` table is used as-shipped — we DO NOT reimplement PAT storage. Our SanctumTokenIssuer decorates Sanctum's `createToken(...)` to cache the compact permission array on the `abilities` column at issue time (so downstream services don't need a per-request RBAC round-trip). firebase/php-jwt is wrapped in JwtSigner + JwtVerifier so callers speak our idiom (kid resolution from `auth_jwt_signing_keys`, deny-list check from `auth_jwt_deny_list`, per-Application `app` claim binding) while the JWT algorithm itself remains the library's.

tenant.description: Tenant-facing authentication surface. Every route below carries the `sanctum` guard (issuance) OR the `sanctum` PAT (post-issuance). `X-Application-Id` header REQUIRED on every request; missing / unknown / disabled application → 400 / 404 / 403 per spec §Req 2.

platform-admin.description: Platform-admin authentication surface + identity-substrate CRUD (identity substrate is UI-less — its admin surface lives here). Every route carries `platform_admin` guard; no `X-Application-Id` header required (platform staff are cross-application by definition).

public.description: Unauthenticated endpoints. Rate-limited by IP + monitored aggressively — aggressive because these are the only Auth surfaces reachable without a bearer token.
